description: Scales a geometric shape uniformly by a given factor around a center point, where factors greater than 1 enlarge and between 0 and 1 shrink.
utility: Scale a shape by a factor around a center point

arguments.name: factor
arguments.description: Scale factor (>1 enlarges, 0-1 shrinks, negative reflects)

arguments.name: cx, cy
arguments.description: Center of scaling as x, y coordinates (default: origin)

arguments.name: centerPoint
arguments.description: Center of scaling as a point expression (alternative to cx, cy)

alternates.description: Scale shape around origin (0,0) by the given factor
Scale shape around explicit center (cx, cy)
Scale shape around a point expression

examples.scenario: Scale a triangle by factor 2 from the origin
examples.expression:
T = sas(G, 3, 60, 3, point(G, 1, 1))
T2 = scale(G, T, 2)

examples.scenario: Shrink a circle by half around its center
examples.expression:
center = point(G, 3, 3)
C = circle(G, 4, center)
C2 = scale(G, C, 0.5, center)

examples.scenario: Scale a polygon around a specific point
examples.expression:
P = polygon(G, point(G, 0, 0), point(G, 4, 0), point(G, 4, 3), point(G, 0, 3))
P2 = scale(G, P, 1.5, 2, 1.5)

examples.scenario: Scale a line segment by factor 3 from origin
examples.expression:
A = point(G, 1, 0)
B = point(G, 2, 0)
L = line(G, A, B)
L2 = scale(G, L, 3)

examples.scenario: Negative scale to reflect and enlarge
